ABOUT HALTER

At Halter, we’re on a mission to enable farmers and graziers to run the most productive and sustainable operations. We’re committed to delivering real change in the world - this isn’t easy, and in truth, we love that it’s hard.

At Halter, we know how much farmers care-for their animals and their pasture. But the tools they rely on haven’t kept up, and we’re on a mission to change that. Halter helps farmers make smarter decisions - fast. We reckon good pasture management is the key to more productive and sustainable farms. More grass. Healthier soil. Happier animals. And more time back for the people doing the work.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for an epic React Native engineer with a product-obsessed mindset and the ability to lead-someone who cares about what we build, and why. This is a rare chance to solve hard mobile problems at scale.



As a lead, you’ll be across our iOS and Android app experience end-to-end-TypeScript, React Native, and the native bridges that make it work. You’ll be hands-on and collaborative; shipping features, improving performance, hardening offline behaviour, and shaping how we work. We care about how you think and act: curious, thoughtful, practical, and someone who takes absolute ownership.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Lead the architecture and implementation of core React Native features, including native modules/bridges where it makes sense.

 - Bring a strong product lens - you’ll work closely with design and product to make smart trade-offs and ship high-impact features fast.

 - Use AI tools (e.g. Claude, Cursor) to boost quality and speed without sacrificing readability or tests.

 - Drive app performance from every angle - The Halter app has a complex mobile product, understanding of how to tune it is an essential skillset.

 - Build resilient offline-first flows and graceful error handling for real-world field conditions.

 - Evolve our mobile design system and UI patterns.Raise the bar through reviews, pairing, and mentoring.
   
   


YOU’LL BE GREAT IF YOU

 - Are deep in React Native and have strong opinions on mobile architecture (navigation, state, module boundaries).

 - Think like a product engineer-customer outcomes first, clean code in service of that.Know when to go native and how to design stable bridges.

 - Have shipped complex React Native apps in cross-functional teams.

To find out more, visit our LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/halter-limited & Instagram https://www.instagram.com/lifeathalter/.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Ever dream about being in tech but aren’t quite ready to trade in your cowboy boots? Look no further than Halter! As the leading provider of virtual fencing technology, Halter is on a mission to enable 50% of the world’s landmass to be more productive and sustainable. This role is paramount for our growth aspirations and will enable us to continue making an impact on ranches across the US.

As a Territory Manager at Halter, you will play a critical role in driving new business growth and ensuring customer success within your designated territory. You will be responsible for executing sales strategies, building strong customer relationships, and meeting ambitious growth targets, all while acting as Halter’s on-the-ground representative. This role requires a proactive and hands-on approach, with a focus on both expansion and long-term customer satisfaction. In this role, you will prioritize daily efforts that optimize performance and drive value for your customers. Working closely with cross-functional teams, including Product, Support, and Customer Onboarding, you’ll share field insights to ensure Halter’s technology continues to meet the needs of US farmers.

Your day-to-day could include being on horseback, helping customers gather their cattle, on a side-by-side traversing their pastures, talking about how Halter could benefit their pastures, or attending industry events like CattleCon or your local Livestock Association meetups.

 

This role covers the the state of Georgia. Frequent travel is required as apart of this role.

 


WHAT YOUR DAY COULD LOOK LIKE

 - Prospecting New Business: You’re driving up driveways, attending local cattle auctions, meeting with your local Livestock, Cattleman's, Stockman's associations, building your new business pipeline through outbound lead generation efforts. You’re fielding inbound leads in a timely manner, prioritizing administrative tasks with your in-field activities.

 - In-Field Sales: You don’t mind getting your hands and boots dirty. Halter is best discussed over a coffee at a kitchen table, in the horse saddle, or on a side-by-side in a pasture. You’re out on the ranch helping potential customers before diving into the value of Halter.

ABOUT THE ROLE

To ensure we can keep the system airtight and continue to deliver on this mission, we’re looking to bring on a Cloud Security Operations Engineer. Halter isn’t a “small SaaS app” - we support hundreds of thousands of devices operating out in the field, paired with a mobile + web product, and relied on daily by farmers. As we scale globally, security becomes less about checklists and more about building the right systems so we can move fast and stay secure.



This is a hands-on role focused on AWS infrastructure security and security operations. You’ll be responsible for improving preventative controls, deploying and operating security tooling across our cloud environment, and strengthening our detection and incident response capability. You’ll also help evaluate and roll out new security technologies (think SIEM/SOAR, better telemetry/log pipelines, higher-signal alerting, and automation) as our SecOps function matures.



You’ll collaborate closely with our Platform team, and partner with Application Security to build security foundations that engineers can actually use - the kind that make the secure path the easy path.

 


WHAT YOUR DAY COULD LOOK LIKE

 - Secure and continuously improve our AWS environment across a mix of managed and self-managed services.

 - Deploy, configure, and operate security tooling on cloud infrastructure (including EC2 instances), improving hardening and visibility.

 - Write and maintain Terraform/IaC to roll out security controls and configuration changes safely and repeatably.

 - Investigate security events and incidents: assess severity and blast radius, drive mitigations, and embed learnings into prevention.

 - Improve incident response readiness: playbooks, alert quality, response workflows, and automations to reduce time-to-detect and time-to-contain.

 - Evaluate and roll out new security capabilities (e.g., SIEM/SOAR, EDR improvements, logging/telemetry), owning the journey from “is this fit for purpose?” to “this is running well in production.”

 - Build security “platform” foundations that teams can consume easily - enabling developers rather than throwing problems over the wall.

Support at Halter isn’t a call centre. It’s a team of problem-solvers and educators who understand our customers, their animals, our product, and how everything connects. We’re the glue between customers and every part of the business, from field teams to product, engineering, and operations across the United States,, Australia, New Zealand, and now South America.

You’ll work both reactively and proactively: jumping in fast when something’s not right, and reaching out when ranchers just need a hand or some guidance. When someone gets in touch, you’ll take ownership from start to finish - listening, understanding what’s needed, and working with teams across Halter to drive the best outcomes on ranch.

Junior Collar Technician: (Fixed Term and/or Permanent)


Roles available in Canterbury and North Otago.


We're building something special at Halter, and we need the right people on the road to make it happen.



As a Junior Field Technician, you'll be a core part of our Fleet Team, keeping our collar fleet moving across farms throughout your dedicated region. You’ll serve as a key connection between what happens on-farm and the engineering team back in Auckland, making sure collars get to the right farms, are tracked accurately, and come back to HQ when needed.


This isn't a sit-at-a-desk role. You'll be out in the field, boots on the ground, driving between farms, working with farmers, and helping deliver one of the most exciting agricultural tech products in the world. It's hard work, but it's meaningful, and it's a genuine foot in the door at one of New Zealand's fastest-growing companies with massive plans for the future of agriculture worldwide.



You'll cover the length and breadth of the country, operating on a continuous farm visit loop. Expect to be on the move and spending up to 2–3 nights away from home per week. Attitude is everything and we are on the hunt for an upbeat go-getter.

Core Requirements:

 - Build your daily travel plan and use route optimisation tools to hit every farm efficiently

 - Hit the road, driving between farms

 - Manage collar stock on customer farms, making sure levels are accurate and nothing slips through the cracks

 - Pack and dispatch urgent spare collars to farms that need them fast

 - Ship collars back to AKL HQ and keep logistics running smoothly

 - Conduct hardware audits and trials on customer farms.

 - Be a positive, professional face of Halter when you're on-farm with our customers


What You Will Bring:

 - Full NZ Class 1 driver's licence — non-negotiable, you'll be clocking serious km's

 - On-farm experience (bonus points if you've worked with cows)

 - A communicator who leaves farmers feeling good about Halter, your interactions matter

 - Physically fit and up for the manual side of the work

 - Reliable, punctual, and someone who takes ownership, if you say you'll be there, you're there

 - A strong work ethic with a track record to back

 - Design custom PCBs from schematic through layout, bring-up, and production handover.

 - Define and execute validation strategy for new and revised hardware (coverage, acceptance criteria, evidence packs).

 - Partner with Machine Design, Test Software, and Production to ensure hardware meets line requirements.

 - Lead root cause analysis for electrical failures in production and test hardware; own durable CAPA.

 - Manage PCB fab and assembly relationships; drive DFM feedback and acceptance testing.

What we're looking for

 - Strong PCB design skills: mixed-signal and power, schematic to layout.

 - Hands-on bring-up and validation experience (oscilloscope, instruments, scripted test).

 - Track record delivering reliable hardware in a production or test environment.

 - Comfortable working across mechanical, software, and firmware boundaries.

Nice to have

 - Firmware/embedded experience: comfortable reading code, debugging hardware-firmware interfaces.

 - Mechanical CAD familiarity: enough to collaborate closely on fixture integration.


JOIN OUR TEAM

Halter is committed to promoting a diverse and inclusive workplace — a place where we can each be ourselves and do the best work of our lives. Research shows that while men apply to jobs when they meet an average of 60% of the requirements, women and under-represented groups of candidates tend to only apply when they meet every requirement. We’d love to chat to see if you’ll be an epic fit!

If this opportunity sounds like you, please apply below by sending through your cover letter explaining why you’re excited about this role and working at Halter, along with your CV, and we’ll be in touch!

Please also feel free to check out the careers page https://www.halterhq.co

ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re changing the way the world farms - and with that comes a responsibility to the producers worldwide who trust us with their animals. We’re looking for a highly capable and first-principled individual to join our Reliability & Failure Analysis team and build quicker, more accurate processes to debug failed hardware to root cause, both from the field and lab testing. Halter collars aren’t serviced, aren’t repairable and need to last 6-10 years, anywhere across the globe. To achieve this, we need to know about every failure in the field and how to eliminate it.


You’ll be responsible for building a team that debugs and diagnoses failed hardware, including collars and tower components, to deeply understand the root cause. Halter products are complex, highly integrated electromechanical devices, and debugging and root-causing the many different failure modes that occur requires highly skilled engineers who are inquisitive and methodical. This is both a technical and operational role where you will be central to closing the feedback loop between our lab & field performance and our engineering teams, ensuring that every failure is investigated, understood, and used to improve product reliability. This is a critical role to enable growth across the globe.


Our aspiration is to resource this team to increase accuracy and throughput, while lifting the deep technical competency and lab capabilities onsite in our Auckland office. This is a huge opportunity for the right person - you will have a lasting impact on farmers across the globe.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Ownership of the returns pipeline end-to-end: own intake, safe handling, triage, diagnostics, storage and traceability. Set clear targets for turnaround time, throughput and root-cause accuracy.

 - Iterate the diagnostics process to improve accuracy and reduce no-fault-founds.

 - Personally contribute to the highest-impact or deeply ambiguous failure modes. This is a hands-on role; you’ll be very close to the product.

 - Complete ownership of the failure analysis lab capability: build a roadmap to bring capabilities in-house, such as X-Ray/CT, cross-section, SEM etc.
